title: Afghan Church
tagline: Afghan Church — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Church of St John the Evangelist, better known as the Afghan Church, is an Anglican church belonging to the Mumbai diocese of the Church of North India.
overview.description: The Church of St John the Evangelist, better known as the Afghan Church, is an Anglican church belonging to the Mumbai diocese of the Church of North India. Located in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India, it was built by the British between 1847 and 1858 to commemorate the dead of the First Afghan War and the disastrous 1842 retreat from Kabul. Memorials at the rear of the nave also record casualties from the Second Anglo-Afghan War. The foundation stone was laid in 1847; it was consecrated in 1858, and work on the steeple finished in 1865.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: INHS Asvini
topPlaces.description: The INHS Asvini is a hospital of the Indian Navy in Mumbai. With 825 beds, the tertiary care institution is the oldest and largest hospital of the Indian Navy.

topPlaces.name: Navy Nagar
topPlaces.description: Navy Nagar is a cantonment area in Mumbai, India, and was established in 1796. The area is owned and managed solely by the Indian Navy and entry to this area is highly restricted.

topPlaces.name: Adarsh Housing Society scandal
topPlaces.description: The Adarsh Housing Society is a 31-story building constructed on prime real estate in Colaba, Bombay, for the welfare of war Widows and personnel of India's Ministry of Defence. Over a period of several years, politicians, bureaucrats and military officers allegedly conspired to bend several rules concerning land ownership, zoning, floor space index and membership getting themselves flats allotted in this cooperative society at below-market rates.

topPlaces.name: Colaba
topPlaces.description: Colaba ([koˈlaːbaː]; or ISO: Kolābā) is a part of the city of Mumbai, India. It is one of the four peninsulas of Mumbai while the other three are Worli, Bandra and Malabar Hill.

topPlaces.name: Colaba Causeway
topPlaces.description: Colaba Causeway, officially known as Shahid Bhagat Singh Road, is a commercial street, and a major causeway or land link between Colaba and the Old Woman's Island in the city of Mumbai, India. It lies close to the Fort area, and to the east of Cuffe Parade, an upmarket neighbourhood in South Mumbai, and close by are Mumbai's famous landmarks, the Gateway of India and Taj Mahal Palace & Tower.

topPlaces.name: Cuffe Parade
topPlaces.description: Cuffe Parade is a historic and significant business district of the city of Mumbai, India. It is home to a collection of commercial and office high-rises.

topPlaces.name: 1947 Ramdas ship disaster
topPlaces.description: The 1947 Ramdas ship disaster occurred near Bombay (now Mumbai) in India. The Indian passenger ship SS Ramdas, while bound for Rewas in Maharashtra, capsized on 17 July 1947, near Gull Island (Kashyacha Khadak), ten miles from Colaba (South Mumbai) Point, killing 724 of the people on board.

topPlaces.name: World Trade Centre, Mumbai
topPlaces.description: The Mumbai World Trade Centre at Cuffe Parade is a skyscraper built in the year 1970. It is the first world trade center in India.

items.note: The INHS Asvini is a hospital of the Indian Navy in Mumbai. With 825 beds, the tertiary care institution is the oldest and largest hospital of the Indian Navy.
Navy Nagar is a cantonment area in Mumbai, India, and was established in 1796. The area is owned and managed solely by the Indian Navy and entry to this area is highly restricted.

The Adarsh Housing Society is a 31-story building constructed on prime real estate in Colaba, Bombay, for the welfare of war Widows and personnel of India's Ministry of Defence. Over a period of several years, politicians, bureaucrats and military officers allegedly conspired to bend several rules concerning land ownership, zoning, floor space index and membership getting themselves flats allotted in this cooperative society at below-market rates.
Colaba ([koˈlaːbaː]; or ISO: Kolābā) is a part of the city of Mumbai, India. It is one of the four peninsulas of Mumbai while the other three are Worli, Bandra and Malabar Hill.

Colaba Causeway, officially known as Shahid Bhagat Singh Road, is a commercial street, and a major causeway or land link between Colaba and the Old Woman's Island in the city of Mumbai, India. It lies close to the Fort area, and to the east of Cuffe Parade, an upmarket neighbourhood in South Mumbai, and close by are Mumbai's famous landmarks, the Gateway of India and Taj Mahal Palace & Tower.
Cuffe Parade is a historic and significant business district of the city of Mumbai, India. It is home to a collection of commercial and office high-rises.

The 1947 Ramdas ship disaster occurred near Bombay (now Mumbai) in India. The Indian passenger ship SS Ramdas, while bound for Rewas in Maharashtra, capsized on 17 July 1947, near Gull Island (Kashyacha Khadak), ten miles from Colaba (South Mumbai) Point, killing 724 of the people on board.
The Mumbai World Trade Centre at Cuffe Parade is a skyscraper built in the year 1970. It is the first world trade center in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Afghan Church?
faq.a: The best time to visit Afghan Church is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Afghan Church?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Afghan Church?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Afghan Church at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Afghan Church?
faq.a: Stays in Afghan Church start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Afghan Church?
faq.a: The top attractions are INHS Asvini, Navy Nagar, Adarsh Housing Society scandal, Colaba, Colaba Causeway.
